rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEILL'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion is denied as to the 1st and 3rd causes of action. These derivative claims are well-pleaded. Defendant owed a fiduciary duty to the entities and a factual issue remains regarding breach, causation, and damages. The Court grants the motion with 15 days leave to amend as to the 14th cause of action. Plaintiffs shall plead facts, if possible, showing how defendant owed a duty to plaintiffs as individuals. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT VLADIMIR ABRAMOV'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473.5. Defendant has shown that he did not receive actual notice of this suit and that his lack of notice was not caused by his avoidance or service or inexcusable neglect. The motion is timely because the evidence does not show that Defendant was ever served with written notice of entry of default. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ECONOMY FOODS INC., A CORPORATION DBA FACCIOLA VS. KOBRA CULINARY CONCEPTS INC., A CORPORATION DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Economy Foods, Inc Motion To Strike Answer And Enter Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2012, Line 6, Plaintiff Economy Foods, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Answer And Enter Default And Default Judgment. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CHEUNG VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion To Quash Records Request And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DAVID CHEUNG'S Motion To Quash Records Request And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is not satisfied that the parties have fully satisfied their obligation to meet and confer with regard to the matters at issue. Accordingly, the parties are directed to meet and confer fully about these discovery issues, including the question of a protective order, prior to the hearing. Counsel will advise the court at the hearing of the progress they have made in narrowing, simplifying and resolving the discovery issues. The court will refrain from issuing a ruling until it has been advised of the results of the parties' required meet and confer session. The conference may take place the morning of the hearing (at 8:00 AM). If necessary, the matter will be placed at the end of the calendar to facilitate the required negotiation. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Proposed Motion And Proposed Motion To Strike Entire Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANTS EDWARD NOVAK, ANTHONY NOVAK'S Motion To Strike Entire Complaint. The motion to strike is denied. The face of the complaint does not show that the complaint violates the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. In addition, defendant fails to cite any authority holding that the Act would apply in the instant circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
